zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Porter I於1540年5月15日出生在英國艾可費爾斯泰德時，他的父親Sir William Porter Jr35歲，母親Margaret Atwood35歲。他於1560年1月12日與Anne Marie Calvert在英國英格蘭藍卡爾吉可哈姆結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1614年12月20日在英國英格蘭艾可蕾托 巴多福享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可蕾托 巴多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Porter I于1540年5月15日出生在英国艾可費爾斯泰德时，他的父亲Sir William Porter Jr35岁，母亲Margaret Atwood35岁。他于1560年1月12日与Anne Marie Calvert在英国英格兰藍卡爾吉可哈姆结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1614年12月20日在英国英格兰艾可蕾托 巴多福享年74岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可蕾托 巴多福。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Porter I was born on 15 May 1540, in Felsted, Essex, England, his father, Sir William Porter Jr, was 35 and his mother, Margaret Atwood, was 35. He married Anne Marie Calvert on 12 January 1560, in Kirkham, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 20 December 1614, in Little Baddow, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in Little Baddow, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Porter I est né le 15 mai 1540, à Felsted, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Porter Jr, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Margaret Atwood, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Anne Marie Calvert le 12 janvier 1560, à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 20 décembre 1614, à Little Baddow, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Little Baddow, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Porter I è nato il il 15 maggio 1540 nel Felsted, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir William Porter Jr, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Atwood, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Anne Marie Calvert il 12 gennaio 1560, nel Kirkham, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 20 dicembre 1614, nel Little Baddow, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Little Baddow, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Porter Iが1540年5月15日にイギリスエセックスフエルステドで生まれた時，父Sir William Porter Jrは35歳，母Margaret Atwoodは35歳であった。1560年1月12日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアキルクハムでAnne Marie Calvertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1614年12月20日にイギリスイギリスエセックスリットル バッドオウで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスリットル バッドオウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Porter I(이)가 1540년 5월 15일 영국 에세스 프스테드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Porter Jr(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Atwood(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1560년 1월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 커크함에서 Anne Marie Calvert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1614년 12월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 바도에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 바도에 묻혔다.

pt.15 May 1540–20 December 1614: 15 de maio de 1540–20 de dezembro de 1614
pt.Little Baddow, Chelmsford, Essex, Englan: Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Little Baddow, Essex, England, United Ki: Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Porter I nasceu em 15 de maio de 1540, em Felsted, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Porter Jr, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Atwood, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Marie Calvert em 12 de janeiro de 1560, em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de dezembro de 1614, em Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Porter I родился 15 мая 1540 г. в местности фелстед, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Porter Jr было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Atwood, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Marie Calvert 12 января 1560 г. в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 20 декабря 1614 г. в местности литл баддоу, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности литл баддоу, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Porter I nació el 15 de mayo de 1540, en Felsted, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Porter Jr, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Margaret Atwood, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Anne Marie Calvert el 12 de enero de 1560, en Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 20 de diciembre de 1614, en Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
